title: Lathe and Turning Machine Tool Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
description: Set up, operate, or tend lathe and turning machines to turn, bore, thread, form, or face metal or plastic materials, such as wire, rod, or bar stock.

tasks.text: Adjust machine controls and change tool settings to keep dimensions within specified tolerances.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Move controls to set cutting speeds and depths and feed rates, and to position tools in relation to workpieces.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Study blueprints, layouts or charts, and job orders for information on specifications and tooling instructions, and to determine material requirements and operational sequences.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect sample workpieces to verify conformance with specifications, using instruments such as gauges, micrometers, and dial indicators.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Replace worn tools, and sharpen dull cutting tools and dies, using bench grinders or cutter-grinding machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Move toolholders manually or by turning handwheels, or engage automatic feeding mechanisms to feed tools to and along workpieces.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Compute unspecified dimensions and machine settings, using knowledge of metal properties and shop mathematics.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Crank machines through cycles, stopping to adjust tool positions and machine controls to ensure specified timing, clearances, and tolerances.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Position, secure, and align cutting tools in toolholders on machines, using hand tools, and verify their positions with measuring instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Start lathe or turning machines and observe operations to ensure that specifications are met.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Refill, change, and monitor the level of fluids, such as oil and coolant, in machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lift metal stock or workpieces manually or using hoists, and position and secure them in machines, using fasteners and hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install holding fixtures, cams, gears, and stops to control stock and tool movement, using hand tools, power tools, and measuring instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select cutting tools and tooling instructions, according to written specifications or knowledge of metal properties and shop mathematics.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mount attachments, such as relieving or tracing attachments, to perform operations, such as duplicating contours of templates or trimming workpieces.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Turn valve handles to direct the flow of coolant onto work areas or to coat disks with spinning compounds.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
